calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-28-2026 Continued To Mar-29-27 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Set For Trial On Oct-12-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Defendant'S Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And His Counsel In The Amount Of $4,000.00
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CLAUDIA GONZALEZ's Motion For Protective Order Limiting Defendant's Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And His Counsel In The Amount Of $4,000.00.

The motion for protective order is denied. The court has reviewed the special interrogatories at issue and concludes that the complexity and numerosity of issues in this case warrants the additional special interrogatories that defendant has propounded. In particular, there are five defendants; there are seven causes of action in the complaint, and the complaint comprises 113 paragraphs. There is modest overlap in some interrogatories but they are distinct and the court concludes that the overlap is not burdensome or harassing.

The court finds Plaintiff's meet and confer efforts to be adequate and the motion to be timely.

Plaintiff shall respond to any interrogatories against which she asserted the 35-interrogatory limit by January 16, 2026.

Both parties seek sanctions. "The court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion for a protective order under this section, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.090, subd. (d).) The court does not find substantial justification and orders partial sanctions reflecting its view of a reasonable amount for this motion. Plaintiff shall pay $1500 to Defendant within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

proposed order submitted.
